items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately. Lithium prices have pu

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Analysts' average price target is $214.65. The company's shares are trading for less than 12 times forward earnings, offering an excellent entry point for long-term investors. The dividend yields an above-average 1.15% at its current share price, with a payout ratio of 46%.

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs, RBC Capital Markets said. Lithium supply is expected to remain tight through 2027. RBC

items.headline: Albemarle's EBITDA to Benefit From Higher Lithium Prices, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle's (ALB) EBITDA will likely benefit from stronger lithium prices in the range of low to mid-$20s per kilogram, RBC Capital Markets said. RBC retained an outperform rating on the stock and increased its price target to $253 from $245.
